```
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-binary @scientific_names.json https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
curl -F names=@scientific_names.txt https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: text/plain' --data-binary @scientific_names.txt https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
```

data.current_summary: Legacy fuzzy name match service (Version 1 - flat format)
data.current_description:
Version 1 - Warning: this method will be removed and users are advised to migrate to the version 2 API v2/species/match.

Fuzzy matches scientific names against the GBIF Backbone Taxonomy with the optional classification provided. If a classification is provided and strict is not set to true, the default matching will also try to match against these if no direct match is found for the name parameter alone.

Additionally, a lookup may be performed by providing the usageKey which will short-circuit the name-based matching and ONLY use the given key, either finding the concept or returning no match.
data.summary_suggestion: Match scientific names against GBIF Backbone (deprecated v1)
data.description_suggestion:
DEPRECATED: This version 1 endpoint will be removed. Migrate to v2/species/match instead.

Fuzzy matches scientific names against the GBIF Backbone Taxonomy. Accepts optional taxonomic classification (kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus) to improve matching accuracy. If strict matching is disabled (default), the service attempts classification-based matching when name-only matching fails. Alternatively, provide a usageKey to bypass name matching and directly retrieve a specific name usage. Returns matched name usage in flat format with confidence scores.

data.current_summary: Parse a list of scientific names
data.current_description:
Parses a list of scientific names supplied as a JSON list, a form request or a plain text file with Unix (\n) line endings.  In all cases the names should use UTF-8 encoding.

```
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-binary @scientific_names.json https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
curl -F names=@scientific_names.txt https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: text/plain' --data-binary @scientific_names.txt https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/parser/name
```

Accepts three content types: application/json (JSON array), multipart/form-data (form field), or text/plain (newline-delimited). Returns an array of ParsedName objects in the same order as input, with each containing parsed name components and any parsing issues detected.
